ontology.foodon_label: mixed grain and protein bowl food product
ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn term for chicken grain bowl with egg; mapped to closest mixed grain and protein bowl concept. FoodOn does not have a specific entry for this Singaporean customisable dish.

main_ingredients: mixed grains (e.g. brown rice, quinoa, barley)
choice of four vegetable or protein toppings
allergens: gluten (possible from sauce or grain mix)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 400 g (one bowl), reflecting a typical restaurant or hawker-style grain bowl portion with chicken, egg, sauce, and four toppings.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken grain bowl with egg (4 toppings) — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for 'Chicken grain bowl with egg (4 toppings)' (CR ID: F08010008). All per-100g macros and micronutrients are taken verbatim from the HPB record. Saturated fat data not reported in the HPB entry; estimated at 1.4 g/100g based on USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for comparable chicken and egg mixed grain dishes (USDA NDB No. 21376 and related composites) and documented as an approximation.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — mixed grain and legume dishes; analogous estimate based on mixed grain bowl (brown rice, barley, quinoa blend) with protein
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific dish. GI of 48 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for mixed grain dishes containing brown rice and barley (GI range 45–52, mean ~48). The protein content (8.76 g/100g) and dietary fibre (2.01 g/100g) from chicken and egg further attenuate the glycaemic response. Glycemic load calculated as: GI (48) × carbohydrate per serving (8.45 g/100g × 400 g serving = 33.8 g) / 100 = 16.2, rounded to 16.3.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 236 mg/100g; approximately 944 mg per 400 g serving, approaching 41% of the 2,300 mg daily limit
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should request sauce on the side and choose low-sodium sauce options to reduce sodium intake per serving; selecting potassium-rich toppings such as spinach or edamame can help offset the sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low estimated GI (~48), moderate carbohydrate (8.45 g/100g), high protein (8.76 g/100g), and dietary fibre (2.01 g/100g) support a blunted postprandial glycaemic response
type2_diabetes.guidance: This dish is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when a whole grain base is selected; the combination of protein, fibre, and low GI grains moderates blood glucose rise, but sauce sugar content should be monitored if teriyaki-based.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate total fat (5.31 g/100g), estimated low saturated fat (~1.4 g/100g), high protein, and dietary fibre from mixed grains and vegetable toppings support a heart-healthy profile
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The lean protein from chicken and egg, combined with fibre-rich mixed grains and four vegetable toppings, supports cardiovascular health; individuals with CVD should request sauce on the side to limit sodium and choose olive oil-based dressings to favour unsaturated fat intake.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Four vegetable toppings increase antioxidant and fibre intake; egg provides vitamin D; absence of deep-frying reduces pro-inflammatory fat exposure
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: This dish is generally suitable for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis; if toppings include nightshade vegetables (tomato, bell pepper, chilli), monitor individual tolerance, and request olive oil-based dressing for enhanced anti-inflammatory benefit.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chicken grain bowl with egg (4 toppings) is a customisable Singaporean mixed dish consisting of cooked chicken, a whole egg, and a sauce served over a base of mixed grains — typically a blend of brown rice, barley, and quinoa — accompanied by a choice of four toppings, which may include vegetables such as broccoli, edamame, corn, spinach, or other seasonal produce. The dish is assembled rather than cooked as a single unit, with each component prepared separately: the chicken is typically grilled, poached, or steamed; the egg is boiled or poached; and the grains are steamed. The texture is varied — tender chicken, firm egg, chewy whole grains, and crisp or soft vegetable toppings — with a savoury flavour profile from the sauce, which may be teriyaki, sesame, or a light soy-based dressing. Nutritionally, the dish provides 117 kcal per 100 g, with a strong protein contribution of 8.76 g/100g from chicken and egg, moderate carbohydrate at 8.45 g/100g from the grain base, and dietary fibre at 2.01 g/100g supporting satiety and glycaemic moderation. Sodium is 236 mg/100g, which is borderline for hypertension management at full serving size. The dish is halal-certified and low-calorie, making it suitable for weight management, Type 2 Diabetes, and cardiovascular health when sauce is controlled. The four-topping version provides greater micronutrient density than the three-topping variant, with increased antioxidant and fibre intake from additional vegetables.

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient and micronutrient values are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (CR ID: F08010008) with a reported confidence of 0.95. The record is marked as reviewed and canonical. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA SR Legacy analogues and documented. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for mixed grain dishes, which is the best available approved source for this composite food.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (1.4 g/100g) is an approximation derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for comparable chicken and egg mixed grain dishes; the HPB source does not report saturated fat for this entry. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this customisable composite dish are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. 3. GI of 48 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for mixed grain dishes (brown rice and barley blend); no direct GI measurement exists for this specific dish. 4. Glycemic load per serving calculated as GI (48) × available carbohydrate per serving (8.45 g/100g × 400 g = 33.8 g) / 100 = 16.2, reported as 16.3. 5. Serving size of 400 g is an estimate for a standard restaurant or hawker-style grain bowl; actual portion weight may vary by vendor. 6. The dish is classified as halal based on the input dietary tags; kosher status is not confirmed. 7. Toppings are variable by customer choice; nutritional values reflect the HPB composite estimate for the 4-topping variant.
